rationale: Recent active-side incident / regional-service batch covering Cetus Protocol, KiloEx, Bybit Japan exit, OKX DEX aggregator suspension, and Paradex. Cetus, KiloEx, and Paradex are DEX/perp DEX entities with major exploit or technical incident events but no terminal shutdown. Bybit and OKX remain active centralized exchanges; this batch records regional/service-level restrictions without treating the global entities as dead. Apply scripts should preserve existing Bybit/OKX entity records if already present and add only the events/evidence for those existing entities.

entities.summary: Sui- and Aptos-based decentralized exchange and liquidity protocol. In May 2025 it suffered a very large exploit that halted trading and paused smart contracts, but it restarted in June 2025 after a recovery plan, frozen-fund transfer, and Sui Foundation support.
entities.notes: Do not mark dead. The May 2025 exploit was severe and temporarily halted trading, but the protocol relaunched in June 2025. HEI records it as active with critical exploit, trading halt, and restart events.

entities.summary: Multi-chain perpetual futures decentralized exchange that suffered an approximately USD 7 million oracle-manipulation exploit in April 2025. Operations were paused and a compensation / resolution plan was later announced, so the entity is tracked as active with major incident events rather than dead.
entities.notes: Do not mark dead. Current evidence supports exploit and temporary operational suspension, followed by a compensation/resolution plan and service-resumption framing.

entities.summary: Centralized exchange launched in 2018 and still operating globally. This batch only records the Japan resident service exit / account restriction events scheduled for 2026, not a global shutdown.
entities.notes: If a Bybit entity already exists, preserve the existing canonical record and append only the Japan-exit events/evidence. The regional restrictions do not change global entity status.

entities.summary: Global centralized exchange formerly known as OKEx. This batch records the March 2025 temporary suspension of OKX's DEX aggregator / DeFi service after Lazarus misuse concerns and regulatory consultation, not a global exchange shutdown.
entities.notes: If an OKX/OKEx entity already exists, preserve the existing canonical record and append only the DEX-aggregator suspension events/evidence. The event is product/service-level and does not change global entity status.

entities.summary: Starknet-based decentralized perpetuals exchange. In January 2026 a database migration / pricing-data error briefly caused BTC to be priced at zero, triggering unintended liquidations and a rare chain rollback with refunds, but the platform was not permanently shut down.
entities.notes: Do not mark dead. Track as active with critical pricing-error, rollback, and refund/compensation events.

events.title: Cetus Protocol suffered a major exploit
events.description: Cetus Protocol suffered a major exploit on May 22, 2025, with reports estimating about USD 223 million to USD 260 million drained from Sui/Aptos liquidity pools and more than USD 160 million of assets later frozen or recovered through the Sui ecosystem response.
events.notes: Loss amount varies across sources; use approximate language.

events.title: Cetus halted trading and paused contracts
events.description: Cetus halted trading / paused smart-contract activity while investigating the exploit and coordinating recovery through Sui validators, the Sui Foundation, and security partners.
events.notes: Temporary platform halt; not a terminal shutdown.

events.title: Cetus relaunched after recovery plan
events.description: Cetus relaunched on June 8, 2025 after recovery work, external audits, frozen-fund transfer approval, a Sui Foundation loan, and liquidity restoration for affected pools.
events.notes: Modeled as other because HEI has no dedicated restart/relaunch enum.

events.title: KiloEx suffered a price-oracle exploit
events.description: KiloEx suffered an approximately USD 7 million price-oracle manipulation exploit across multiple chains, including Base, BNB Chain, and Taiko exposure.
events.notes: Use exploit because the incident exploited oracle pricing logic rather than a general exchange-wallet breach.

events.title: KiloEx operations were paused after the exploit
events.description: KiloEx paused operations / trading for roughly ten days while preparing recovery and compensation measures after the oracle-manipulation attack.
events.notes: Temporary service disruption; not a shutdown.

events.title: KiloEx announced compensation and resolution plan
events.description: KiloEx published a resolution plan to compensate users with open positions and address trading-position imbalances before service resumption.
events.notes: Compensation/resolution context supporting active rather than dead classification.

events.title: Bybit announced Japan resident service restrictions
events.description: Bybit announced that it would discontinue services for Japanese residents and gradually apply account restrictions from 2026 in response to Japanese regulatory-compliance requirements, after earlier suspension of new Japan registrations.
events.notes: Regional exit only; Bybit global entity remains active.

events.title: Bybit Japan user verification deadline reached
events.description: Affected users were instructed to complete Identity Verification Level 2 / proof-of-address checks by January 22, 2026 or be classified as Japanese residents and face service limitations.
events.notes: Modeled as other because this is a regional KYC / residency-classification milestone rather than a global exchange status event.

events.title: OKX paused its DEX aggregator after Lazarus misuse concerns
events.description: OKX temporarily suspended its decentralized exchange aggregator after consulting regulators, citing efforts to prevent further misuse by Lazarus-linked activity and to apply security upgrades during heightened EU/MiCA scrutiny of its Web3 service.
events.notes: Product/service-level event only. OKX global exchange remains active.

events.title: OKX wallet service remained available while new wallet creation was limited in some markets
events.description: Reporting stated that OKX Wallet remained available to customers while new wallet creation was paused in selected markets as part of the service-review and upgrade response.
events.notes: Supporting service-scope event; not a full wallet or exchange shutdown.

events.title: Paradex pricing glitch triggered mass liquidations
events.description: A database migration / pricing-data error on Paradex briefly priced BTC at zero during maintenance, triggering mass unintended liquidations across perpetual markets.
events.notes: Use trading_halted because the incident affected trading, liquidation, and platform state.

events.title: Paradex performed a chain rollback
events.description: Paradex rolled back chain state to a prior block after the pricing-data incident, a rare and controversial response to a technical failure in a decentralized perpetuals platform.
events.notes: Modeled as other because HEI has no rollback-specific event enum.

events.title: Paradex refunded affected users after the rollback incident
events.description: Paradex said it refunded about USD 650,000 to around 200 users after the maintenance-related bug and unintended liquidations.
events.notes: Refund context supports active classification despite the critical incident.

evidence.title: Sui DEX Cetus Protocol Halts Trading After $200M Liquidity Drain In Hack
evidence.notes: Used for initial trading halt, pool drain, token impact, and approximate $200M+ exploit framing.

evidence.title: Sui DEX Cetus Protocol restarts platform after recovering from $223 million exploit
evidence.notes: Used for relaunch, recovered/frozen funds, Sui Foundation loan, and affected-pool restoration details.

evidence.title: Sui Dex Cetus Protocol Restarts Platform Weeks After $230M Exploit
evidence.notes: Supporting source for relaunch, community vote, frozen funds, and reimbursement plan.

evidence.title: DEX KiloEx Loses $7M in Apparent Oracle Manipulation Attack
evidence.notes: Used for exploit type, approximate $7M loss, date, and multi-chain exposure.

evidence.title: KiloEX users promised compensation after $7 million price oracle exploit
evidence.notes: Used for compensation plan, 10-day pause, and service-resumption context.

evidence.title: KiloEx hack: DEX recovers $7M, reveals compensation plan
evidence.notes: Supporting source for recovery/compensation and platform-resumption framing.

evidence.title: Bybit to Exit Japan in 2026 Over Regulatory Compliance Issues
evidence.notes: Used for Japan resident service-discontinuation framing, account restrictions from 2026, and regulatory-compliance context.

evidence.title: Bybit Ends Services for Japanese Residents, Implements Account Restrictions
evidence.notes: Supporting source for December 22 announcement date, gradual restrictions, and January 22 verification deadline.

evidence.title: Bybit to Exit Japan in 2026 Over Regulatory Compliance Issues
evidence.notes: Secondary mirror confirming Japan resident restrictions and Identity Verification Level 2 deadline.

evidence.title: OKX suspends DEX aggregator to stop further misuse by Lazarus
evidence.notes: Used for OKX's DEX aggregator suspension, Lazarus misuse statement, and regulatory-consultation wording.

evidence.title: Crypto Exchange OKX Suspends DeFi Service After EU Scrutiny
evidence.notes: Used for EU scrutiny / Bybit hack laundering context and service-suspension framing.

evidence.title: OKX Halts DEX Aggregator Services Amid Lazarus Group Misuse and EU Investigations
evidence.notes: Supporting source for wallet-scope limits, continued wallet availability, and DEX aggregator suspension details.

evidence.title: Paradex's free bitcoin pricing glitch triggers mass liquidations, forces rollback
evidence.notes: Primary source for BTC-zero pricing glitch, mass liquidations, and planned rollback.

evidence.title: Paradex refunds $650K to 200 users after maintenance bug triggers liquidations
evidence.notes: Used for post-mortem, race-condition / maintenance-bug explanation, refunds, and user impact count.

evidence.title: Bitcoin Price Crashes to Zero on Paradex Exchange as Glitch Fuels Mass Liquidations
evidence.notes: Supporting source for chain rollback to block 1604710, affected API/bridge/explorer context, and BTC-zero incident chronology.

manual_review_checklist: Confirm no existing Cetus Protocol, KiloEx, Paradex records before canonical apply.
If Bybit exists, preserve the entity and append regional Japan exit events/evidence only.
If OKX/OKEx exists, preserve the entity and append DEX aggregator suspension events/evidence only.
Keep all five entities active; none are dead-side records.
Use event ids hei_ev_000369 through hei_ev_000381 only if available.
Use evidence ids hei_src_000775 through hei_src_000789 only if available.
